title: AirHandlingUnit
description: AirHandlingUnit: Air Handling Units are devices which house fans, filter, coils, and energy wheels which produce heated and cooled fresh/partially recirculated air to a building

Type.description: Type denotes the kind of Air Handling Unit (eg heat and ventilation, energy recovery)

TotalAirFlow.description: Total Airflow accounts for the maximum amount of supply/outside air that the Air Handling Unit will be passing. This value is measured in m3/s (e.g. 2.35 m3/s (5000 CFM) of Outside Air)

AirVelocityAcrossCoil.description: Air velocity across coil denotes the maximum velocity of the air that should be passed along the Air Handling Unit's coil. This value is measured in m/s (maximum values around 2.5 m/s (500 ft/min) are considered best practice)

Parts.description: A collection of the parts (Air Handling Unit, Fans, Coils, Energy Wheel, Filters, Electrical Connectors) that make up the Air Handling Unit

_bhomVersion.description: Optional version of BHoM used as part of automatic versioning and schema upgrades.
